name: Stone Town
description: UNESCO World Heritage old town of Zanzibar City, famous for its winding alleys, carved wooden doors, bazaars and Swahili-Arab-Indian architecture.

name: Forodhani Gardens
description: Seafront garden in Stone Town that becomes a lively night food market each evening, known for Zanzibar pizza, grilled seafood and sugarcane juice.

name: Old Fort (Ngome Kongwe)
description: The oldest building in Stone Town, a 17th-century Omani fort that now hosts craft stalls, an amphitheatre and cultural events.

name: House of Wonders
description: Landmark waterfront palace (Beit-al-Ajaib) in Stone Town, historically the first building in Zanzibar to have electricity and a lift.

name: Darajani Market
description: Stone Town's main bazaar for spices, fruit, fish and everyday goods - a busy, authentic local market.

name: Prison Island (Changuu)
description: Small island a short boat ride from Stone Town, home to a colony of giant Aldabra tortoises and good snorkelling.

name: Nungwi Beach
description: Lively white-sand beach at Zanzibar's northern tip, known for sunsets, dhow building, diving and nightlife.

name: Kendwa Beach
description: Relaxed northern beach near Nungwi with calm swimming water unaffected by tides and famous full-moon parties.

name: Paje Beach
description: Wide east-coast beach famous for kitesurfing, turquoise lagoon and a laid-back backpacker scene.

name: Jambiani Beach
description: Quiet traditional fishing village on the southeast coast with long white sands and seaweed farming.

name: Jozani Forest
description: Jozani-Chwaka Bay National Park, the only place to see the endemic Zanzibar red colobus monkey, plus mangrove boardwalks.

name: Mnemba Atoll
description: Protected coral atoll off the northeast coast, Zanzibar's top spot for snorkelling and diving with dolphins and turtles.

name: The Rock Restaurant
description: Iconic restaurant perched on a rock in the sea off Michamvi, reachable on foot at low tide or by boat at high tide.

name: Kuza Cave
description: Natural turquoise freshwater swimming cave near Jambiani with a small cultural centre.

name: Kizimbani Spice Farm
description: Working spice farm near Stone Town offering guided tours of cloves, nutmeg, cinnamon, vanilla and tropical fruit.

name: Mtoni Palace Ruins
description: Ruins of the oldest palace in Zanzibar on the coast north of Stone Town, once home to the sultan's family.

name: Cheetah's Rock
description: Small ethical animal sanctuary near Nungwi offering close interactive tours; booking ahead is required.

name: Michamvi Peninsula
description: Scenic peninsula on the east coast known for sunset-facing west beaches, calm bays and quiet luxury resorts.

name: Nakupenda Sandbank
description: A pristine sandbar that appears at low tide near Stone Town, popular for picnic boat trips and snorkelling.

name: Mangapwani Beach
description: Quiet northwest beach with a historic coral slave cave nearby and clear swimming water.
